name: University Library
description: The main university library, housing extensive collections across all subjects with study spaces, IT facilities, and a café.

name: Arts A
description: Home to the School of Media, Arts and Humanities, with teaching rooms and academic offices.

name: Arts A Lecture Theatres
description: Also known as the Asa Briggs Lecture Theatres, housing large lecture spaces for arts and humanities.

name: Arts B
description: Teaching and office space for the School of Media, Arts and Humanities.

name: Arts C
description: Additional teaching and research space for the School of Media, Arts and Humanities.

name: Arundel
description: Academic building housing teaching rooms, seminar spaces, and offices for several departments.

name: Ashdown House
description: Contains offices and teaching spaces for science and engineering departments.

name: Attenborough Centre for the Creative Arts (ACCA)
description: A multi-arts venue presenting performances, exhibitions, and events. Named after Richard Attenborough, the university's first Chancellor.

name: Bramber House
description: A central campus hub with conference facilities, a restaurant, and the campus shop.

name: Chichester I
description: Teaching and seminar rooms serving multiple academic departments.

name: Chichester II
description: Houses lecture rooms, teaching spaces, and departmental offices.

name: Chichester III
description: Academic building with teaching rooms and office space.

name: Chichester Lecture Theatre
description: A dedicated lecture theatre serving the Chichester teaching complex.

name: Essex House
description: Contains offices for professional services and administration.

name: Falmer House
description: One of the original Basil Spence buildings (1962). Houses student services and the Students' Union shop.

name: Fulton
description: Named after physicist John Fulton. Contains teaching rooms and academic offices.

name: Boiler House
description: The campus energy centre, also containing some teaching and workshop spaces.

name: Institute of Development Studies (IDS)
description: A world-leading centre for research, teaching, and learning on international development.

name: Jubilee
description: Built to celebrate the university's 25th anniversary. Houses academic offices and teaching rooms.

name: Jubilee Lecture Theatre
description: A large lecture theatre adjacent to the Jubilee building, used for major lectures and events.

name: Meeting House
description: A multi-faith space and social hub, one of the original Basil Spence campus buildings.

name: Norwich House
description: Contains offices for professional services and academic support.

name: Richmond
description: Academic building with teaching rooms and departmental offices.

name: Shawcross
description: Houses academic offices and teaching spaces for several departments.

name: Silverstone
description: Contains teaching rooms and offices, located near the central campus area.

name: Student Centre
description: The main Students' Union building with social spaces, bars, and student society offices.

name: Sussex House
description: University administrative headquarters, housing senior management and central services.

name: Accelerator Building
description: A modern research facility supporting innovation and enterprise activities.

name: Aisin Seiki
description: Named after its donor. Houses engineering and design research facilities.

name: Biomedical Research Facility
description: Dedicated facility for biomedical and life sciences research.

name: BSMS Research
description: Research building for the Brighton and Sussex Medical School, a joint venture between the two universities.
aliases: Brighton and Sussex Medical School Research

name: BSMS Teaching
description: Teaching building for the Brighton and Sussex Medical School, with clinical skills labs and lecture theatres.
aliases: Brighton and Sussex Medical School Teaching

name: Centre for Research Perception and Cognition (CRPC)
description: Research centre focused on perception, cognition, and neuroscience.

name: Clinical Imaging Sciences Centre (CISC)
description: Advanced imaging facility with MRI and other scanning equipment for clinical research.

name: Freeman
description: The Freeman Centre houses computing and engineering facilities with modern lab spaces.

name: Friston
description: Academic building with teaching rooms and offices for science departments.

name: Genome Damage and Stability Centre (GDSC)
description: A research centre studying genome integrity, DNA repair, and their links to cancer.

name: Hastings
description: Contains teaching rooms and academic offices for science and engineering.

name: John Clifford West
description: Houses research laboratories and offices for science departments.

name: John Maynard Smith (JMS)
description: Named after the renowned evolutionary biologist. Houses the School of Life Sciences.

name: Old Ancillary Building
description: Support building near the science campus area.

name: Pevensey I
description: Part of the Pevensey complex, housing the School of Mathematical and Physical Sciences.

name: Pevensey II
description: Contains computing labs, teaching spaces, and the School of Engineering and Informatics.

name: Pevensey III
description: Additional teaching and lab space within the Pevensey science complex.

name: Sussex Health Outcomes Research and Education in Cancer (SHORE-C)
description: Research centre focused on cancer health outcomes, supportive care, and patient experience.

name: Sussex Innovation Centre
description: An award-winning business incubator supporting start-ups and growing companies.

name: Thermo-Fluid Mechanics Research Centre (TFMRC)
description: Specialist research centre for thermo-fluid mechanics and turbomachinery.

name: Trafford Centre
description: Houses research groups and postgraduate teaching spaces.

name: Health Centre
description: The University Health Centre providing NHS GP and nursing services to students and staff.

name: Health & Wellbeing Centre
description: Provides counselling, mental health support, and wellbeing services for the university community.

name: Sport Centre
description: The main campus sports facility with a gym, swimming pool, sports halls, and fitness classes.

name: Falmer Sports Complex
description: Outdoor sports facilities including pitches, courts, and a 3G playing field.

name: Co-operative Childcare
description: A nursery providing childcare for children of university staff and students.

name: The Keep
description: An archive and records centre shared by the university, East Sussex Record Office, and Brighton council.

name: Brighthelm
description: Student residential accommodation located on the north side of campus.

name: East Slope - Amberley
description: Modern student residences on the East Slope, opened in 2020. Named after the West Sussex village.

name: East Slope - Bodiam
description: Modern student residences on the East Slope, opened in 2020. Named after Bodiam Castle.

name: East Slope - Camber
description: Modern student residences on the East Slope, opened in 2020. Named after Camber Sands.

name: East Slope - Lullington
description: Modern student residences on the East Slope, opened in 2020. Named after Lullington Heath.

name: Lewes Court
description: Large student residential complex on the north edge of campus, offering en-suite rooms.

name: Northfield
description: Student residential accommodation located at the northern boundary of campus.

name: Stanmer Court
description: Student residences near Falmer Station, popular with first-year students.

name: Swanborough
description: Student residential area with nearby laundrette and social facilities.

name: Falmer Station
description: The railway station serving the campus, with trains to Brighton, Lewes, and London.
